full_response:
To find the components of the tensor $-6v^a u^b$, we first understand that $v^a u^b$ represents the tensor product of the two vectors $v^a$ and $u^b$. This results in a rank-2 tensor where each component is the product of the corresponding components of $v^a$ and $u^b$. Then, we multiply each component by the scalar factor -6.

Let $T^{ab}$ denote the tensor $-6v^a u^b$. The indices $a$ and $b$ range from 0 to 3.

Given the components of the vectors in frame R:
$v^a = (v^0, v^1, v^2, v^3) = (5, -1, 0, 1)$
$u^a = (u^0, u^1, u^2, u^3) = (-2, 1, 1, -6)$

We will calculate each of the $4 \times 4 = 16$ components $T^{ab} = -6 v^a u^b$:

For $a=0$:
$T^{00} = -6 v^0 u^0 = -6 \times (5) \times (-2) = -6 \times (-10) = 60$
$T^{01} = -6 v^0 u^1 = -6 \times (5) \times (1) = -6 \times 5 = -30$
$T^{02} = -6 v^0 u^2 = -6 \times (5) \times (1) = -6 \times 5 = -30$
$T^{03} = -6 v^0 u^3 = -6 \times (5) \times (-6) = -6 \times (-30) = 180$

For $a=1$:
$T^{10} = -6 v^1 u^0 = -6 \times (-1) \times (-2) = -6 \times 2 = -12$
$T^{11} = -6 v^1 u^1 = -6 \times (-1) \times (1) = -6 \times (-1) = 6$
$T^{12} = -6 v^1 u^2 = -6 \times (-1) \times (1) = -6 \times (-1) = 6$
$T^{13} = -6 v^1 u^3 = -6 \times (-1) \times (-6) = -6 \times 6 = -36$

For $a=2$:
$T^{20} = -6 v^2 u^0 = -6 \times (0) \times (-2) = 0$
$T^{21} = -6 v^2 u^1 = -6 \times (0) \times (1) = 0$
$T^{22} = -6 v^2 u^2 = -6 \times (0) \times (1) = 0$
$T^{23} = -6 v^2 u^3 = -6 \times (0) \times (-6) = 0$

For $a=3$:
$T^{30} = -6 v^3 u^0 = -6 \times (1) \times (-2) = -6 \times (-2) = 12$
$T^{31} = -6 v^3 u^1 = -6 \times (1) \times (1) = -6 \times 1 = -6$
$T^{32} = -6 v^3 u^2 = -6 \times (1) \times (1) = -6 \times 1 = -6$
$T^{33} = -6 v^3 u^3 = -6 \times (1) \times (-6) = -6 \times (-6) = 36$

The components of $-6v^a u^b$ can be represented as a $4 \times 4$ matrix, where the first index is the row and the second index is the column:

$$
T^{ab} = \begin{pmatrix}
T^{00} & T^{01} & T^{02} & T^{03} \\
T^{10} & T^{11} & T^{12} & T^{13} \\
T^{20} & T^{21} & T^{22} & T^{23} \\
T^{30} & T^{31} & T^{32} & T^{33}
\end{pmatrix}
$$

Substituting the calculated values:

$$
\boxed{
-6v^a u^b = \begin{pmatrix}
60 & -30 & -30 & 180 \\
-12 & 6 & 6 & -36 \\
0 & 0 & 0 & 0 \\
12 & -6 & -6 & 36
\end{pmatrix}
}
$$

Confidence: 100%
